title: pilosella brachiata subsp. amblyphylla
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. amblyphylla, also known as Wood Sorrel or Alpine Wood-sorrel, is a rare subspecies of the Pilosella brachiata plant, typically found in mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Pilosella brachiata subsp. amblyphylla have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions, with its health benefits including anti-oxidant and anti-bacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
